edge_hypothesis: ema_rsi_atr is the system's rising star: 2 ACCEPTs, ranging PF=2.864 (nearly matching champion Supertrend at 2.914). Both ACCEPTs use narrow RSI bands — the precision_10to1 variant uses 50-65 range, ea9p5k2m uses narrow bands too. This spec pushes in TWO unexplored directions simultaneously: (1) Ultra-narrow RSI bands (48-62 long, 38-52 short) to catch the very START of momentum shifts when RSI is near neutral — before other indicators react. The thesis: when EMA9>EMA21 (trend established) and RSI is near 50 (no extreme yet) and ATR is elevated (volatility present), a new momentum wave is just beginning. (2) SOL as test asset — completely untested on ANY template. SOL has higher beta than ETH, more frequent ranging periods, and wider price swings. The ATR volatility gate (ATR > 20-bar ATR MA) should be especially discriminating on SOL where volatility spikes are sharper. If ema_rsi_atr works on SOL, we double our addressable market without changing signal logic. If ultra-narrow bands work, it validates the 'catch it early' thesis and could be applied to all templates. Invalidation: PF < 1.0 on SOL = asset doesn't suit the EMA/RSI/ATR combo. PF < 1.0 with narrow bands on ETH = bands are too narrow and catch noise, not signal.

variants.name: ema_rsi_cascade_sol_8to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on SOL/4h with ultra-narrow RSI bands (48-62 long, 38-52 short) and 8:1 R:R. First-ever ema_rsi_atr test on SOL. The ultra-narrow bands (RSI 48-62) target entries when RSI is near neutral — the very beginning of a momentum shift. Combined with EMA9>EMA21 (trend direction) and ATR > ATR_MA_20 (volatility present), this catches early momentum cascades. SOL's higher beta should produce more volatile continuations from these early entries.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 on SOL, the asset's microstructure doesn't suit EMA/RSI/ATR signals — stick to ETH
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 > EMA_21 for trend direction on SOL — first SOL test for this template
RSI_14 in ultra-narrow band 48-62 (long) / 38-52 (short) — catches early momentum before RSI reaches extremes
ATR_14 > 20-bar ATR MA volatility gate + 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP for 8:1 R:R

variants.name: ema_rsi_cascade_sol_10to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on SOL/4h with moderately narrow RSI bands (45-58 long, 42-55 short) and 10:1 R:R. Slightly wider bands than the ultra variant for more entries. 10:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 20.0x TP) captures the full SOL volatility tail. If SOL's higher beta produces longer momentum runs, 10:1 should outperform 8:1 on this asset even if it underperforms on ETH.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < ema_rsi_cascade_sol_8to1, SOL doesn't produce long enough tails for 10:1 — keep 8:1
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 > EMA_21 trend direction — same as ultra variant
RSI_14 in moderately narrow band 45-58 (long) / 42-55 (short) — slightly wider for more entries
ATR_14 volatility gate + 2.0x stop / 20.0x TP — 10:1 R:R to capture SOL's longer volatility tails

variants.name: ema_rsi_cascade_eth_1h_7to1
variants.description: ema_rsi_atr on ETH/1h with ultra-narrow RSI bands (48-62 long, 38-52 short) and 7:1 R:R. Both existing ACCEPTs are on ETH/4h. This tests ultra-narrow bands on 1h for higher trade frequency. 1h should produce 3-4x more entries than 4h, giving a larger sample. 7:1 R:R (1.5x stop, 10.5x TP) is calibrated for 1h noise — tighter stop to cut losers fast, wide enough TP to capture intraday momentum cascades.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (10.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < existing 4h ACCEPTs (1.288-1.327), 1h degrades ema_rsi_atr signal quality — stay on 4h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 10.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 > EMA_21 on 1h — higher frequency trend detection
RSI_14 ultra-narrow 48-62 (long) / 38-52 (short) — same thesis as SOL variant, tested on proven ETH at 1h
ATR_14 volatility gate + 1.5x stop / 10.5x TP — 7:1 R:R calibrated for 1h noise
